2606.29069.title: Low-cost concept-based localized explanations: How far can we get with training-free approaches?
2606.29069.paper_summary: Concept-based Explainable AI (C-XAI) seeks human-understandable explanations grounded in semantic concepts, yet validation is limited by the scarcity of fine-grained concept annotations. We evaluate whether mid-scale Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) can perform localized concept naming under strict zero-shot conditions by assigning labels to bounding-box regions at both object and part levels. We propose a reproducible zero-shot evaluation protocol for Concept Naming (CoNa) with (i) closed-set, category-constrained prompting for moderate vocabularies and (ii) Open-CoNa, an embedding-similarity-based strategy for large label spaces. Experiments with four MLLMs (7B-32B) show consistent performance trends across datasets, reaching 62%-88% object-level exact-match accuracy, highlighting the potential of training-free concept annotation from localized regions. We discuss limitations and failure modes and release a reproducible framework to support future low-cost C-XAI research.
2606.29069.paper_summary_zh: 概念基礎的可解釋人工智慧（C-XAI）尋求基於語義概念的人類可理解解釋，但由於細粒度概念標註的稀缺，驗證受到限制。<lb/>我們評估中型多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）是否能在嚴格的零樣本條件下執行局部概念命名，通過為物體和部件層級的邊界框區域分配標籤。<lb/>我們提出了一個可重複的零樣本概念命名（CoNa）評估協議，包括（i）針對中等詞彙的封閉集、類別約束提示，以及（ii）Open-CoNa，一種基於嵌入相似性的策略，用於大型標籤空間。<lb/>對四個MLLMs（7B-32B）的實驗顯示，跨數據集的一致性能趨勢，達到62%-88%的物體層級精確匹配準確率，突顯了從局部區域進行無需訓練的概念標註的潛力。<lb/>我們討論了限制和失敗模式，並釋放了一個可重複的框架以支持未來低成本的C-XAI研究。
2606.29069.author: Darian Fernández-Gutiérrez et.al.
2606.29069.authors: Darian Fernández-Gutiérrez, Rafael Bello, Marilyn Bello, Natalia Díaz-Rodríguez
